top_signals.title: US 30-Year Yield Raises Alarm in Longest Run Above 5% Since 2007
top_signals.summary: The US 30-year bond yield has held above 5% for the longest stretch since the dawn of the financial crisis, echoing investor concerns about a growing debt pile and sticky inflation.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Upper House panel approves referendum reform bill
top_signals.summary: The bill to revise the law was backed by the ruling coalition as well as several opposition parties.
